method_atom: A technique for finding stationary, constant-flux cascade solutions of a nonlinear kinetic (collision-integral) equation governing the irreversible pairwise merging of discrete units, where the merging rate is set by a homogeneous interaction kernel and a conserved additive quantity is injected at the small-scale end. A scaling/conformal (Zakharov-type) transformation of the collision integral exposes power-law (Kolmogorov-type) spectra that carry a constant flux of the conserved quantity from small to large scales; a locality criterion derived from the asymptotic scaling of the kernel selects which power law is admissible, and the finite flux-capacity of the spectrum diagnoses whether a runaway condensation into an unbounded aggregate occurs.
object_atom: The cluster-mass distribution of coagulating particles described by the Smoluchowski aggregation equation with a source injecting small masses, evaluated for the homogeneous kernel family K_ζ(m1,m2)=(m1 m2)^(ζ/2) spanning both gelling and non-gelling coalescence regimes.
brief_reason: The constant-flux (Kolmogorov) cascade, Zakharov-transform solution method, locality criterion, and capacity-based runaway diagnosis carry across as field-neutral machinery, while the coalescing-mass/coagulation system and its specific kernel are the aggregation-physics-specific object.
